exemplar.title: In what ways and to what effect does Ali Wong's stand-up comedy special on Netflix, "Hard Knock Wife", employ specific stand-up comedy techniques to impact perceptions of gender roles through an intersectional feminist lens?

detailedExemplarData.title: In what ways and to what effect does Ali Wong's stand-up comedy special on Netflix, "Hard Knock Wife", employ specific stand-up comedy techniques to impact perceptions of gender roles through an intersectional feminist lens?

subject.title: English A Lang & Lit
subject.description:
**IB English A Language & Literature** is a **Group 1** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **the study of language and literature through a wide variety of texts and media from different periods, styles, and cultures**, with a strong focus on **critical analysis of both literary and non-literary texts, examining how language choices create meaning and effect in different contexts**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Paper 1** | Text analysis | 1h 15m | 2h 15m | 35% (SL), 25% (HL) |
| **Paper 2** | Comparative essay | 1h 45m | 1h 45m | 35% (SL), 25% (HL)  |
| **HL Essay** | Written assignment | N/A | 1200-1500 words | 25% (HL only) |
| **Individual Oral** | Oral presentation | 15 minutes | 15 minutes | 30% (SL), 20% (HL) |

strengths: The research question is clearly stated, focused, and consistently integrated into every section of the essay, ensuring a coherent line of inquiry.
Topic communication is accurate and effective, with a clear introduction and thematic headings that reinforce purpose and focus.
Methodological choices (selection of Ali Wongâs Netflix special and intersectional feminist lens) are generally appropriate and demonstrate informed source selection.
weaknesses: The methodology lacks an explicit dedicated section and rationale for secondary source and scene selection, which undermines methodological transparency.
Range of scholarly sources is somewhat limited, and criteria for choosing Canada, France, and Germany for policy comparison are not fully articulated.
Transitions between some thematic sections are abrupt, reducing the smoothness of the argumentative flow.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Two strands (topic clarity and research question focus) demonstrate top-band performance (band 5-6), with the methodology strand falling into band 3-4 due to limited explicitness and range. On balance, the work sits securely in the upper half of the 5-6 band but does not fully meet the methodological rigor for the top mark, justifying a score of 5.

strengths: Accurate and consistent use of subject-specific terminology (e.g., âintersectional feminism,â âsatire,â âmotherhood penaltyâ) throughout the analysis.
Demonstrates coherent and nuanced understanding of both stand-up comedy conventions and feminist theory, integrating scholarly definitions (Crenshaw; Miller & Bassett).
Effectively applies contextual knowledge (e.g., linking Asian cultural norms to motherhood expectations) to enrich the argument.
weaknesses: Secondary sources are occasionally used illustratively rather than analytically, limiting depth in some sections (e.g., sexual autonomy discussion).
Minor lapses in precision (e.g., misattribution in a figure caption) detract slightly from overall analytical accuracy.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student shows excellent command of terminology and a coherent understanding of the topic, with effectiveâbut not consistently deepâuse of sources. This performance exceeds the middle band but falls just short of the exemplary, warranting a mid-band 5 in a 5-6 range.

strengths: Analysis is detailed and insightful, with strong scene-by-scene commentary linking performance techniques to feminist critique (e.g., vocal imitation, hyperbole).
Research is generally relevant and supports most analytical claims, drawing from both primary performance and secondary commentaries.
Argument culminates in a clear, evaluative conclusion that reflects on comedyâs transformative power and policy implications.
weaknesses: Engagement with academic literature on stand-up theory is uneven, limiting the breadth of research to mid-band relevance.
Critical evaluation of alternative interpretations and limitations is present but not fully developed, reducing the sophistication of the argumentâs evaluation.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The essay exhibits analysis that is securely in the 7-9 band and research and evaluation that sit in the 4-6 band. Overall performance aligns with the upper half of the 7-9 band without fully reaching the sustained excellence of the top tier, justifying a score of 8.

strengths: Structure fully meets IB requirements, with a clear table of contents, thematic subheadings, and logical progression.
Layout and formatting adhere to EE conventions, including consistent font, margins, spacing, and correctly formatted in-text citations and reference list.
Visual materials are integrated and captioned, aiding reader comprehension and supporting the analysis.
weaknesses: Figure captions lack standardized panel labels and complete source credits, requiring refinement to IB conventions.
Inconsistent heading capitalization and occasional dense paragraphs reduce visual uniformity and readability.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Despite minor inconsistencies in figure labeling and paragraphing, the report meets all core IB presentation requirements and demonstrates clear, appropriate structure and formatting. This warrants the maximum score of 4.

strandSelections.reasoning: The student identifies one primary text and supplements it with a modest selection of secondary sources (transcript website, UN Women explainer, academic articles on motherhood bias).  Citations are generally correct and there is evidence of informed selection; however, the range of scholarly material is limited and no explicit methodological rationale (e.g., criteria for scene selection or theoretical framework beyond general intersectionality) is provided.  Consequently, the methodology is **mostly complete but not consistently rigorous**, fitting the 3-4 band.

Formatting follows IB EE conventions: consistent font, margin, and spacing, correct in-text citations, and a properly formatted reference list.  Visual materials are captioned and integrated.  Minor inconsistencies (e.g., variable figure labelling) are inconsequential, so **layout and formatting meet the full set of requirements**, warranting the top band.

Research draws on the primary performance and an assortment of secondary commentaries that are **generally relevant and sufficiently broad to address the research question**.  While stronger engagement with academic literature on stand-up comedy theory would have elevated the work, the sources chosen do support most analytical claims, placing the strand in the mid-band (4-6).

The student opens with a clear, concise title and an explicit statement of purpose, immediately signalling an investigation into Ali Wongâs use of stand-up techniques to shape perceptions of gender roles.  Throughout the essay the focus on stand-up comedy as a vehicle for intersectional feminist critique is maintained and is reiterated at the start of every major subsection.  The consistent, purposeful connection between each analytical point and the overarching theme demonstrates **accurate and effective communication of the topic with a sustained sense of direction**.

The discussion culminates in a clear conclusion that restates findings and asserts comedyâs transformative power.  Some evaluative comments (e.g., critique of U.S. maternity policy) show reflection, yet **critical evaluation of the essayâs own limitations or alternative readings is thin**.  Logical flow is evident but not consistently sophisticated, matching the middle band.

The student offers detailed scene-by-scene commentary that links performance devices to feminist effects, often supported by visual evidence and quotations.  **Analytical insights are usually well-reasoned and extend beyond description**, though at times repetition and reliance on anecdotal interpretation reduce depth.  Overall, the analysis moves past adequacy into the âgoodâ range of the 7-9 band, albeit nearer the lower end.

The essay includes all required EE components: title page with word count, abstract equivalent introduction, analytical body organised by thematic subheadings, conclusion, references, and a clear table of contents with page numbers.  Transitions between sections are smooth, and the structure supports the argument effectively; **there are no significant omissions**, justifying placement in the highest presentation band.

The essay demonstrates **good knowledge of both stand-up comedy conventions and intersectional feminist theory**, applying terms such as âshock humour,â âraw storytelling,â and âmotherhood penaltyâ accurately.  The student references relevant statistics and scholarly commentary, but the secondary material is occasionally thin and sometimes used illustratively rather than analytically, preventing a fully coherent, expertly nuanced treatment that would characterise the top band.

Specialised terminologyââintersectional feminism,â âsatire,â âhyperbole,â âvoice imitation,â âphysical comedyââis **largely accurate and integrated naturally** into the analysis.  Minor lapses in precision (e.g., interchangeable use of âtechniqueâ and âstrategyâ) appear, but overall the vocabulary shows a solid command of Language & Literature concepts, aligning with the 3-4 band descriptors.

The research question is explicitly stated on the title page and reframed in the introduction, limiting the scope to one Netflix special (*Hard Knock Wife*) and specifying both the methodological focus (stand-up techniques) and the theoretical lens (intersectional feminism).  All analytical sections consistently reference this question, showing **a clear, well-focused line of inquiry that is strongly integrated into the discussion**.

annotations.comment: Clarify why you selected Canada, France, and Germany for comparison in your methodology, outlining criteria that inform this choice.
The inclusion of a precise word count at the start demonstrates adherence to formal formatting requirements and enhances professional presentation.
Figure caption lacks panel labels and standard source-credit format; refine to IB conventions by labeling sub-figures (a, b) and specifying author and timestamp.
Your analysis of vocal imitation and facial expression in Figure 1 compellingly ties performance technique to feminist critique, showing strong analytical depth.
Your commentary on physical comedy effectively deepens the analysis by highlighting how exaggerated gestures satirize caregiving stereotypes.
Your hyperbole analysis (âBellagio fountainâ) insightfully connects comedic exaggeration to critique of capitalist undervaluation of maternal labor.
The research question is explicitly stated and tightly focused, specifying both the primary text and theoretical lens, which ensures a coherent link throughout the analysis.
This introduction effectively contextualizes Ali Wongâs work and frames the study within intersectional feminism, establishing a clear purpose and focus.
The title precisely conveys the investigationâs focus and sets a clear, purposeful direction for the essay, effectively anchoring the reader in the topic and research scope.
The table of contents is well structured, providing clear navigation through sections and reinforcing the essayâs logical organization.

The methodology is integrated implicitly under analysis; consider adding an explicit âMethodologyâ heading in the table of contents to foreground your analytic process and criteria for scene selection.
The citation âVagdevi Meunier, 2024â is not fully detailed in the reference list; verify consistency and completeness of all source entries.
The incorporation of scholarly definitions of intersectionality (Crenshaw; Miller & Bassett) demonstrates good command of subject-specific concepts and effective use of sources.
While the theoretical framework is clear, the introduction lacks a concise methodological roadmap; consider specifying your criteria for selecting scenes and secondary sources.
The âStand-up Comedy Techniquesâ section doubles as method and analysis; consider separating a dedicated methodology subsection to clarify your analytical framework.
The discussion of raw storytelling is precise, linking anecdotal technique to broader societal critique and demonstrating solid knowledge of both comedic conventions and feminist theory.
The thematic heading âMotherhood Stereotype: Career and Family Expectationâ clearly signals the sectionâs focus and reinforces the essayâs cohesive structure.
The intersectional layer linking Asian cultural norms to motherhood expectations enriches your argument and demonstrates effective use of contextual knowledge.
Section heading capitalization (âBreaking the romanticized imageâ) is inconsistent with earlier headings; apply uniform formatting for a cleaner layout.
Caption misattributes the performer as Margaret Cho rather than Ali Wong; correct the image attribution to maintain analytical accuracy.
The discussion of breastfeeding as âchronic physical tortureâ and its satirical framing shows precise application of feminist concepts to performance analysis.
Figures 4 & 5 are not individually labeled (a, b) and lack uniform captioning; revise numbering and styling to satisfy IB presentation standards.
Figure 6 & 7 caption should list each figure with its own number and timestamp for clarity; adjust to conform with figure-caption conventions.
Interpreting âThe Giving Treeâ as a commentary on maternal exhaustion demonstrates creative critical evaluation and adds depth to your discussion.
The transition into âWorkplace Discriminationâ is abrupt; include a linking sentence to reinforce how this new theme connects back to your research question.
The comparative policy analysis effectively highlights systemic gender inequities, strengthening the relevance of your argument to the research question.
The satirical critique of C-sections would be enriched by engaging with medical sociology research to substantiate your claims and broaden source relevance.
The heading for âGender Expectations & Parenting Double Standardsâ clearly signals a major thematic shift and aids logical flow.
This dense paragraph covers multiple themes; consider dividing it into shorter paragraphs to improve readability and maintain focus.
Self-deprecating humor as analysis of postpartum recovery compellingly validates lived experiences and fosters audience empathy.
The blog source citation lacks publisher information and retrieval date; complete reference details to ensure reliability and academic rigor.
The section on sexual autonomy is insightful but lacks engagement with key scholarly works on feminist sexuality; integrate academic sources to deepen context.
Reference formatting is inconsistent (mixture of narrative and URL styles); standardize citations to a single style guide to enhance professionalism.
The analysis of subverting cultural and gender norms demonstrates accurate use of terminology and solid understanding of feminist satire.
The conclusion restates findings effectively but omits discussion of study limitations and avenues for future research; consider adding to strengthen critical evaluation.
